turns.content:
I've been reading about defmt! macros and RTT transport but I still don't fully understand how it actually works in a no_std context. I'm trying to build a tool to automate some repetitive tasks on embedded hardware but my current approach with panic hooks feels too fragile.

That makes sense but I'm still confused about how the formatting actually happens without std::fmt. Can you explain what defmt::Format trait does and how it connects to the RTT transport layer? Also, I'm frustrated because my current setup keeps crashing when I try to use panic! instead of logging.

The defmt::Format trait is essentially a custom formatting protocol that allows your code to emit log events without needing std::fmt. When you call defmt! macros like defmt::info!("status: ok"), the compiler generates a function that takes a format string and arguments, then passes them to the RTT transport layer which handles serialization over serial. The RTT transport layer is what actually sends these messages from your embedded device to your host toolchain via probe-rs integration,
